input.final_summary:
I ran `npm install` and `npm run build` in `C:\Users\User\.cursor\projects\empty-window` with cwd set correctly, but the shell returned **no stdout or stderr** for either command and reported completion in **0 ms** (including a `ping` sanity check), so the captured output and timing are not trustworthy even though both runs reported **exit code 0**.

Please run the same commands locally in that folder (or have the parent agent re-run with a working terminal / full permissions) to get real install/build logs and any TypeScript errors. I could not extract TypeScript diagnostics from this environment.

content.text:
## Command results

Both commands were invoked with working directory `C:\Users\User\.cursor\projects\empty-window`.

### 1. `npm install`

| Field | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Exit code** | `0` |
| **stdout** | *(empty)* |
| **stderr** | *(empty)* |
| **Duration (reported)** | 0 ms |

### 2. `npm run build`

| Field | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Exit code** | `0` |
| **stdout** | *(empty)* |
| **stderr** | *(empty)* |
| **Duration (reported)** | 0 ms |
| **TypeScript errors** | None reported (no build output captured) |

### Reliability note

The execution environment did not return any command output (including `echo hello` and `ping -n 3`), and every invocation finished in 0 ms. That strongly suggests output was not captured or commands did not run as expected hereâ€”not that `npm install` / `npm run build` genuinely completed silently in zero time.

**Recommendation:** Run in your own terminal:

```powershell
Set-Location "C:\Users\User\.cursor\projects\empty-window"
npm install
npm run build
```

If the build fails, paste the full terminal output so TypeScript errors can be analyzed. I can retry from a parent agent session with a working shell if you want that done automatically.
